package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_enough: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Gin handlers that use ShouldBindBodyWith(...) even though only one body bind is observed.
type CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughModel
}

type CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughService struct {
    repo *CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnough(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseShouldbindbodywithWhenSingleBindIsEnoughDTO) error {
    focus := "shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is"
    _ = focus
    raw, _ := c.GetRawData()
    var body map[string]any
    c.ShouldBindJSON(&body)
    _ = raw
    for _, item := range items { db.Create(&item) }
    shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive := map[string]int{"shouldbindbodywith": 1, "when": 2, "single": 3, "bind": 4, "is": 5, "enough": 6}
    shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    shouldbindbodywith_positive_0 := shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["shouldbindbodywith"] + len(input)
    _ = shouldbindbodywith_positive_0
    when_positive_1 := shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["when"] + len(input)
    _ = when_positive_1
    single_positive_2 := shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["single"] + len(input)
    _ = single_positive_2
    bind_positive_3 := shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["bind"] + len(input)
    _ = bind_positive_3
    is_positive_4 := shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["is"] + len(input)
    _ = is_positive_4
    enough_positive_5 := shouldbindbodywith_when_single_bind_is_positive["enough"] + len(input)
    _ = enough_positive_5
    return nil
}
